request_text: Do we need further actions to ensure that kitty-ops op record JSONL files always get committed? The concern: when spec-kitty ask or spec-kitty do is invoked, it writes a kitty-ops/<op-id>.jsonl file that is meant to be a durable record and should be committed (per .gitignore convention which only excludes ops-index.jsonl). Currently there is no automated mechanism to stage or commit these files — it relies on the agent noticing them in git status and committing manually. Should we add a git hook, a post-invocation reminder, or some other mechanism to prevent these from being silently left uncommitted?
